theory.intro: Volume is the amount of space a solid takes up. For a rectangular prism (box), multiply the three dimensions together.
facts: Volume is measured in cubic units: cm$^{3}$, m$^{3}$.
A cube has equal edges, so its volume is **edge $\times$ edge $\times$ edge**.
steps: Read the length, width and height (use the same unit).
Write the answer with cubic units.
